en_definition: used to introduce a reason for an argument
example1: Given the fact that funding is limited, the project must be scaled down.
example1_ru: Учитывая тот факт, что финансирование ограничено, проект необходимо сократить.
example2: Given the fact that she studied hard, her success is no surprise.

en_definition: because of
example1: The trip was cancelled on account of bad weather.
example1_ru: Поездку отменили по причине плохой погоды.
example2: On account of the strike, all trains were delayed.

en_definition: considering; because of
example1: In view of recent events, the policy has been revised.
example1_ru: Ввиду недавних событий политика была пересмотрена.
example2: In view of your grades, we recommend an easier course load.

en_definition: regardless of, not affected by a particular factor
example1: All students must submit their essays irrespective of extensions granted.
example1_ru: Все студенты обязаны сдать эссе независимо от предоставленных продлений.
example2: The rule applies to everyone irrespective of their year of study.

en_definition: taking everything relevant into account before reaching a judgment
example1: All things considered, the experiment was a success.
example1_ru: Принимая всё во внимание, эксперимент можно считать успешным.
example2: All things considered, the trip was worth the cost.

en_definition: only if a particular requirement is met
example1: You may retake the exam on condition that you attend the review session.
example1_ru: Вы можете пересдать экзамен при условии посещения занятия по повторению материала.
example2: The loan was approved on condition that it be repaid within a year.

en_definition: for the reason that
example1: The application was rejected on the grounds that it was incomplete.
example1_ru: Заявка была отклонена на том основании, что она была неполной.
example2: He appealed on the grounds that the exam was unfair.

en_definition: in contrast with; rather than
example1: The study focuses on long-term effects, as opposed to short-term ones.
example1_ru: Исследование сосредоточено на долгосрочных эффектах, в отличие от краткосрочных.
example2: Choose quality as opposed to quantity when selecting sources.

en_definition: used to show a difference between two things being compared
example1: By contrast, the second experiment produced much clearer results.
example1_ru: В отличие от этого, второй эксперимент дал гораздо более чёткие результаты.
example2: By contrast, tuition fees at private universities are much higher.

ru_translation: в равной степени, точно так же
en_definition: used to introduce a point of similar importance
example1: Equally important is the need for accurate data collection.
example1_ru: В равной степени важна необходимость точного сбора данных.
example2: Both approaches are equally valid for this type of research.

en_definition: the total amount of money spent on food shopping
example1: Their monthly grocery bill increased due to rising prices.
example1_ru: Их ежемесячный счёт за продукты увеличился из-за роста цен.
example2: Buying in bulk can reduce your grocery bill.

en_definition: an arrangement to pay for something in several smaller payments over time
example1: You can pay the tuition fee through an installment plan.
example1_ru: Вы можете оплатить обучение через план рассрочки платежей.
example2: The store offers an installment plan for expensive laptops.

en_definition: affordable and not expensive
example1: The student union recommends several budget-friendly restaurants near the campus.
example1_ru: Студенческий союз рекомендует несколько экономичных ресторанов рядом с кампусом.
example2: Look for budget-friendly textbook options at the used bookstore.

en_definition: to spend more money than planned or than one can afford
example1: It's easy to overspend during the first weeks at university.
example1_ru: Легко потратить сверх бюджета в первые недели учёбы в университете.
example2: She overspent her allowance buying new textbooks.

en_definition: to gradually collect money for a particular purpose
example1: He is saving up for a new laptop before the semester starts.
example1_ru: Он копит деньги на новый ноутбук перед началом семестра.
example2: Try to save up a little every month for emergencies.

ru_translation: мелкие расходы, наличные на текущие нужды
en_definition: a small amount of money kept for minor, everyday expenses
example1: The department keeps petty cash for small office supplies.
example1_ru: На кафедре хранятся деньги на мелкие расходы для канцелярских принадлежностей.
example2: Use the petty cash fund for emergency purchases only.

en_definition: a document listing goods or services provided and requesting payment
example1: The supplier sent an invoice for the lab equipment.
example1_ru: Поставщик прислал счёт-фактуру за лабораторное оборудование.
example2: Pay the invoice within thirty days to avoid late fees.

en_definition: money paid before goods or services are received
example1: An advance payment is required to secure your place in the program.
example1_ru: Для закрепления места в программе требуется предоплата.
example2: The advance payment is non-refundable after the deadline.

en_definition: an extra charge added to the usual cost
example1: A surcharge applies for paying tuition by credit card.
example1_ru: За оплату обучения кредитной картой взимается дополнительная плата.
example2: The airline added a surcharge for extra luggage.

en_definition: something that is worth the price paid for it
example1: The meal plan offers good value for money for busy students.
example1_ru: План питания предлагает хорошее соотношение цены и качества для занятых студентов.
example2: This laptop provides great value for money for the price.

en_definition: a reduced price given when buying a large quantity of something
example1: The bookstore offers a bulk discount on sets of textbooks.
example1_ru: Книжный магазин предлагает оптовую скидку на комплекты учебников.
example2: Buying stationery together can qualify for a bulk discount.

en_definition: a written promise from a seller to repair or replace a faulty product
example1: The laptop comes with a two-year warranty covering most repairs.
example1_ru: На ноутбук предоставляется двухлетняя гарантия, покрывающая большинство ремонтов.
example2: Keep your receipt to claim the warranty if needed.

en_definition: having a fault; not working correctly
example1: She returned the defective charger for a replacement.
example1_ru: Она вернула неисправное зарядное устройство для замены.
example2: The store replaced the defective item without any charge.

en_definition: the rules a shop has for exchanging purchased items
example1: Check the store's exchange policy before buying a gift.
example1_ru: Проверьте политику обмена товара в магазине, прежде чем покупать подарок.
example2: The exchange policy allows returns within thirty days.

en_definition: the legal protections given to people who buy goods and services
example1: Consumer rights protect you if a product turns out to be faulty.
example1_ru: Права потребителей защищают вас, если товар оказывается бракованным.
example2: Know your consumer rights before making a big purchase.

en_definition: competition or conflict between brothers and sisters
example1: Sibling rivalry is common but usually fades with age.
example1_ru: Соперничество между братьями и сёстрами — обычное явление, но обычно оно проходит с возрастом.
example2: Their parents tried to reduce sibling rivalry at home.

en_definition: family members beyond parents and children, such as grandparents and cousins
example1: Her extended family gathers every year for the holidays.
example1_ru: Её расширенная семья собирается каждый год на праздники.
example2: In many cultures, extended family plays a big role in raising children.

en_definition: a difference in attitudes and understanding between older and younger people
example1: The generation gap sometimes leads to disagreements about technology.
example1_ru: Разрыв поколений иногда приводит к разногласиям по поводу технологий.
example2: Open conversation can help bridge the generation gap.

en_definition: the way a child is cared for and taught while growing up
example1: Her strict upbringing shaped her disciplined study habits.
example1_ru: Её строгое воспитание сформировало её дисциплинированные привычки к учёбе.
example2: Different cultures have very different approaches to upbringing.

en_definition: a person who relies on someone else for financial support
example1: The form asks whether you have any dependents.
example1_ru: В форме спрашивается, есть ли у вас иждивенцы.
example2: Health insurance often covers dependents as well.

en_definition: a person legally responsible for the care of someone else, especially a minor
example1: The application requires a parent or guardian's signature.
example1_ru: Для заявки требуется подпись родителя или опекуна.
example2: Her legal guardian attended the school meeting.

en_definition: no longer close to or in contact with a family member
example1: He became estranged from his family after moving abroad.
example1_ru: Он отдалился от своей семьи после переезда за границу.
example2: Reconnecting with estranged relatives can be emotionally difficult.

en_definition: to care for and help someone or something develop
example1: Good teachers nurture curiosity in their students from the very first lesson.
example1_ru: Хорошие учителя взращивают любознательность у своих учеников с самого первого урока.
example2: The program aims to nurture young talent in science.

en_definition: a person who watches a sport or event
example1: Thousands of spectators watched the university championship from the main stadium.
example1_ru: Тысячи зрителей наблюдали за университетским чемпионатом с главного стадиона.
example2: The stadium was full of enthusiastic spectators.

en_definition: an official who ensures a sports game follows the rules
example1: The referee stopped the match due to bad weather.
example1_ru: Судья остановил матч из-за плохой погоды.
example2: Players must respect the referee's decisions.

en_definition: the ability to keep doing something difficult for a long time
example1: Marathon runners need excellent endurance to finish such a long race.
example1_ru: Марафонцам необходима отличная выносливость, чтобы завершить такую длинную дистанцию.
example2: The training program builds both strength and endurance.

en_definition: light exercise done before intense physical activity
example1: Always do a proper warm-up before running to avoid injury.
example1_ru: Всегда делайте правильную разминку перед бегом, чтобы избежать травм.
example2: The coach led a ten-minute warm-up before practice.

en_definition: physical or mental strength to sustain effort over time
example1: Studying for long exams also requires a lot of mental stamina.
example1_ru: Подготовка к длительным экзаменам также требует большой психической выносливости.
example2: Regular training improves your stamina over time.

en_definition: a feeling of loyalty and cooperation among members of a team
example1: Group projects can help build team spirit among classmates.
example1_ru: Групповые проекты могут помочь развить командный дух среди одноклассников.
example2: The coach praised the players for their strong team spirit.

en_definition: fair and honest behavior in sport, especially toward opponents
example1: Good sportsmanship means congratulating the winning team even after a loss.
example1_ru: Хорошая спортивная этика подразумевает поздравление команды-победителя даже после поражения.
example2: The award recognized her excellent sportsmanship during the tournament.

en_definition: a competitor thought unlikely to win
example1: The underdog team surprised everyone by winning the match.
example1_ru: Команда-аутсайдер удивила всех, выиграв матч.
example2: Everyone was rooting for the underdog in the final round.

en_definition: a person or team that finishes in second place in a competition
example1: She was the runner-up in the science fair last year.
example1_ru: В прошлом году она заняла второе место на научной ярмарке.
example2: The runner-up still received a certificate and a small prize.

en_definition: a booklet explaining how to use or assemble something
example1: Read the instructions manual before turning on the device.
example1_ru: Прочитайте инструкцию по эксплуатации перед включением устройства.
example2: The instructions manual includes a troubleshooting section.

en_definition: done in a series of clear, ordered stages
example1: The lab handout gives step-by-step instructions for the experiment.
example1_ru: В раздаточном материале лаборатории даются пошаговые инструкции к эксперименту.
example2: Follow the step-by-step guide to assemble the equipment.

en_definition: before, in a formal way
example1: Prior to submitting the form, check all the details carefully.
example1_ru: Перед подачей формы внимательно проверьте все данные.
example2: Please arrive prior to the start of the exam.

en_definition: to make certain that something happens or is true
example1: Ensure the equipment is switched off before leaving the lab.
example1_ru: Убедитесь, что оборудование выключено, прежде чем покинуть лабораторию.
example2: The teacher ensures that every student understands the instructions.

en_definition: to look at or mention as a source of information
example1: Refer to the appendix for the full list of formulas.
example1_ru: Обратитесь к приложению для получения полного списка формул.
example2: The essay refers to several key studies on the topic.

en_definition: to act according to a rule, request, or instruction
example1: Students must comply with the exam room regulations.
example1_ru: Студенты обязаны соблюдать правила проведения экзамена.
example2: The lab equipment complies with international safety standards.

en_definition: following a rule, agreement, or standard
example1: The report was written in accordance with university guidelines.
example1_ru: Отчёт был написан в соответствии с руководящими принципами университета.
example2: Grades are assigned in accordance with the official rubric.

en_definition: to behave according to a particular rule or belief
example1: All participants must adhere to the code of conduct.
example1_ru: Все участники обязаны придерживаться кодекса поведения.
example2: The experiment adhered strictly to the approved protocol.

en_definition: to make small changes to a document or law to improve it
example1: The committee voted to amend the university's attendance policy.
example1_ru: Комитет проголосовал за внесение поправок в политику посещаемости университета.
example2: Please amend the report to include the missing data.

en_definition: additional material attached to the end of a document
example1: See the annex for detailed statistical tables referenced in the report.
example1_ru: Смотрите приложение для получения подробных статистических таблиц, упомянутых в отчёте.
example2: The annex includes the full survey questionnaire.

en_definition: a particular section of a legal or formal document
example1: Read every clause of the contract carefully before signing.
example1_ru: Внимательно прочитайте каждый пункт договора перед подписанием.
example2: The refund clause explains when you can get your money back.

en_definition: to explain or state something formally and clearly in a document
example1: The handbook sets out the rules for laboratory conduct.
example1_ru: В руководстве изложены правила поведения в лаборатории.
example2: The contract sets out the responsibilities of each party.

en_definition: to formally commit to doing something, especially a task or duty
example1: Students undertake independent research in their final year.
example1_ru: На последнем курсе студенты берут на себя проведение самостоятельного исследования.
example2: By signing, you undertake to follow the code of conduct.

en_definition: mentioned or named before, earlier in the text
example1: The aforementioned deadline applies to all applicants without any exceptions.
example1_ru: Вышеупомянутый срок распространяется на всех заявителей без исключений.
example2: Please refer to the aforementioned document for details.

en_definition: in accordance with; as required by a rule or agreement
example1: Pursuant to university policy, all fees must be paid in full.
example1_ru: Согласно политике университета, все сборы должны быть оплачены полностью.
example2: The decision was made pursuant to the committee's guidelines.

en_definition: by this letter or document, formally
example1: I hereby confirm my attendance at the ceremony.
example1_ru: Настоящим я подтверждаю своё присутствие на церемонии.
example2: The university hereby grants permission for the exchange program.

en_definition: acting or speaking for someone else
example1: I am writing on behalf of the student council.
example1_ru: Я пишу от имени студенческого совета.
example2: She accepted the award on behalf of her research team.

en_definition: the person whose signature appears at the end of a document
example1: The undersigned agrees to the terms stated above.
example1_ru: Нижеподписавшийся соглашается с вышеизложенными условиями.
example2: Both parties are represented by the undersigned witnesses.

en_definition: having no legal force; not valid
example1: The agreement becomes null and void if either party breaks it.
example1_ru: Соглашение становится недействительным, если одна из сторон его нарушает.
example2: Any changes made without approval are considered null and void.

en_definition: a formal agreement that must legally be followed
example1: Signing the lease creates a binding agreement between tenant and landlord.
example1_ru: Подписание договора аренды создаёт юридически обязывающее соглашение между арендатором и арендодателем.
example2: Read the terms carefully before entering a binding agreement.

en_definition: legally responsible for something
example1: The university is not liable for lost personal property.
example1_ru: Университет не несёт ответственности за утерянное личное имущество.
example2: Tenants are liable for any damage to the apartment.

en_definition: the rules that a person must accept to use a service or agreement
example1: Read the terms and conditions before signing up for the platform.
example1_ru: Прочитайте условия использования, прежде чем регистрироваться на платформе.
example2: By clicking accept, you agree to the terms and conditions.

en_definition: the state of keeping information private and secret
example1: The survey guarantees full confidentiality for all participants.
example1_ru: Опрос гарантирует полную конфиденциальность для всех участников.
example2: Researchers must respect the confidentiality of personal data.

en_definition: to make previously secret information known
example1: Researchers must disclose any conflicts of interest before publishing their work.
example1_ru: Исследователи обязаны раскрывать любые конфликты интересов перед публикацией своей работы.
example2: The company was required to disclose its financial records.

en_definition: a legal contract that prevents sharing certain confidential information
example1: Interns are asked to sign a non-disclosure agreement.
example1_ru: Стажёров просят подписать соглашение о неразглашении.
example2: The non-disclosure agreement protects the company's research data.

en_definition: a document signed to formally agree to something, such as participating in research
example1: Participants must sign a consent form before the study begins.
example1_ru: Участники должны подписать форму согласия перед началом исследования.
example2: The consent form explains how your data will be used.

en_definition: a group that reviews research to ensure it meets ethical standards
example1: The ethics committee approved the study after a full review.
example1_ru: Этический комитет одобрил исследование после полной проверки.
example2: All human research requires ethics committee approval.

en_definition: to remove information that could identify a person from data
example1: All survey responses were anonymized before analysis to protect privacy.
example1_ru: Все ответы на опрос были обезличены перед анализом для защиты конфиденциальности.
example2: Anonymizing the data protects participants' privacy.

en_definition: agreement given after fully understanding the risks and details of something
example1: Researchers must obtain informed consent before starting the interview.
example1_ru: Исследователи обязаны получить информированное согласие перед началом интервью.
example2: Informed consent ensures participants understand the study's purpose.

en_definition: a study in which neither researchers nor participants know who receives the treatment
example1: The medicine was tested using a double-blind study.
example1_ru: Лекарство тестировалось с помощью двойного слепого исследования.
example2: A double-blind study helps prevent bias in the results.

en_definition: a substance with no medical effect given to compare against a real treatment
example1: The control group received a placebo instead of the actual medicine.
example1_ru: Контрольная группа получала плацебо вместо настоящего лекарства.
example2: Some patients improve even when given a placebo.

en_definition: a study that observes the same subjects repeatedly over a long period
example1: The longitudinal study tracked the same students for ten years.
example1_ru: Лонгитюдное исследование отслеживало одних и тех же студентов на протяжении десяти лет.
example2: Longitudinal studies help reveal long-term trends.

en_definition: a study that observes different subjects at a single point in time
example1: The cross-sectional study compared students from different years at once.
example1_ru: Поперечное исследование сравнивало студентов разных курсов одновременно.
example2: A cross-sectional study is faster than a longitudinal one.

en_definition: a small preliminary study conducted to test a method before the main research
example1: A pilot study helped identify problems with the questionnaire.
example1_ru: Пилотное исследование помогло выявить проблемы в анкете.
example2: Results from the pilot study guided changes to the main experiment.

en_definition: research carried out in a natural setting rather than a laboratory
example1: The field study examined bird behavior in their natural habitat.
example1_ru: Полевое исследование изучало поведение птиц в их естественной среде обитания.
example2: Field studies can be harder to control than lab experiments.

en_definition: a statistical study combining results from many separate studies
example1: The meta-analysis reviewed data from fifty separate studies.
example1_ru: Метаанализ объединил данные из пятидесяти отдельных исследований.
example2: A meta-analysis can reveal patterns not visible in single studies.

en_definition: the ability of a study to produce the same results when repeated
example1: Reproducibility is essential for confirming scientific findings across different laboratories.
example1_ru: Воспроизводимость необходима для подтверждения научных результатов в разных лабораториях.
example2: Poor reproducibility raises doubts about the original study.

en_definition: the extent to which a study's results can be attributed to the variable being tested, not other factors
example1: Poor controls can reduce the internal validity of an experiment.
example1_ru: Слабый контроль может снизить внутреннюю достоверность эксперимента.
example2: The researchers redesigned the study to improve its internal validity.

en_definition: the extent to which findings can be applied to other situations
example1: The small sample size limits the generalizability of the findings.
example1_ru: Небольшой размер выборки ограничивает возможность обобщения результатов.
example2: Researchers discussed the generalizability of their conclusions.

en_definition: to refer to where information came from in academic writing
example1: You must cite a source whenever you use someone else's idea.
example1_ru: Вы обязаны цитировать источник всякий раз, когда используете чужую идею.
example2: Remember to cite a source for every statistic in your essay.

en_definition: a short reference within the text pointing to a source in the bibliography
example1: Add an in-text citation after each quoted sentence.
example1_ru: Добавьте внутритекстовую ссылку после каждого цитируемого предложения.
example2: The professor checked the essay for correct in-text citations.

en_definition: a list of all sources cited in an academic paper, usually at the end
example1: Include a complete reference list at the end of your report.
example1_ru: В конце отчёта укажите полный список литературы.
example2: The reference list must follow the required citation style.

en_definition: to add short explanatory notes to a text
example1: Annotate the article as you read to highlight key points.
example1_ru: Делайте пометки в статье по мере чтения, чтобы выделить ключевые моменты.
example2: The bibliography includes annotated summaries of each source.

en_definition: a note placed at the end of a chapter or document rather than the bottom of the page
example1: Check the endnotes for the full citation of each source.
example1_ru: Смотрите концевые сноски для полной ссылки на каждый источник.
example2: This journal uses endnotes instead of footnotes.

en_definition: reusing your own previous work without proper acknowledgment
example1: Submitting the same essay twice can be considered self-plagiarism.
example1_ru: Сдача одного и того же эссе дважды может считаться самоплагиатом.
example2: Check the policy to avoid accidental self-plagiarism.

en_definition: the quality of being new, unique, and not copied
example1: The thesis was praised for its originality and depth of analysis.
example1_ru: Диссертацию похвалили за оригинальность и глубину анализа.
example2: Plagiarism checkers assess the originality of submitted work.

en_definition: a signed statement confirming that submitted work is a student's own
example1: Attach a declaration of authorship to your final dissertation.
example1_ru: Приложите декларацию об авторстве к вашей итоговой диссертации.
example2: Signing the declaration of authorship confirms the work is original.

en_definition: the ethical standard of producing original work and avoiding cheating
example1: The university takes academic honesty very seriously in all its programs.
example1_ru: Университет очень серьёзно относится к академической честности во всех своих программах.
example2: Violating academic honesty can result in expulsion.

en_definition: secret cooperation, especially to cheat or gain unfair advantage
example1: The university treats collusion between students as a serious offense.
example1_ru: Университет рассматривает сговор между студентами как серьёзное нарушение.
example2: Both students were penalized for collusion on the assignment.

en_definition: to supervise students during an examination to prevent cheating
example1: Two staff members invigilate the exam room at all times.
example1_ru: За экзаменационной аудиторией постоянно следят два сотрудника.
example2: The teacher who invigilates the exam checks student identification.

en_definition: improper behavior during an examination, such as cheating
example1: Exam misconduct can lead to automatic failure of the module.
example1_ru: Нарушение на экзамене может привести к автоматическому провалу модуля.
example2: Any suspected exam misconduct is reported to the department.

en_definition: the tendency for grades to rise over time without a real increase in achievement
example1: Some critics argue that grade inflation makes degrees less meaningful.
example1_ru: Некоторые критики утверждают, что инфляция оценок делает дипломы менее значимыми.
example2: The department monitors data to prevent grade inflation.

en_definition: a method of adjusting grades based on the overall distribution of scores in a class
example1: The professor uses curve grading for the most difficult exams.
example1_ru: Профессор использует оценивание по кривой распределения для самых сложных экзаменов.
example2: Curve grading can raise your grade if the exam was very hard.

en_definition: an average that gives different importance to different values
example1: Your final grade is a weighted average of coursework and exams.
example1_ru: Ваша итоговая оценка — это средневзвешенное значение курсовой работы и экзаменов.
example2: Calculate the weighted average using the given percentages.

en_definition: a grade expressed as a letter, such as A, B, or C
example1: The syllabus explains how percentages convert to a letter grade.
example1_ru: В учебной программе объясняется, как проценты переводятся в буквенную оценку.
example2: She received a letter grade of A for her final project.

en_definition: a course graded only as pass or fail, without a numeric or letter grade
example1: She took the elective as a pass/fail course to reduce pressure.
example1_ru: Она прошла факультатив по системе зачёт/незачёт, чтобы снизить нагрузку.
example2: Pass/fail courses do not affect your grade point average.

en_definition: to attend a class without receiving credit or a grade
example1: He decided to audit a course on ancient history for fun.
example1_ru: Он решил прослушать курс по древней истории просто для удовольствия.
example2: Auditing a course still requires attending lectures regularly.

en_definition: the process of applying credits earned at one institution toward a degree at another
example1: Credit transfer allows students to count courses taken abroad.
example1_ru: Перезачёт кредитов позволяет студентам засчитывать курсы, пройденные за рубежом.
example2: Check the credit transfer policy before studying at another university.
